2604.10884.title: Ambiguity Detection and Elimination in Automated Executable Process Modeling
2604.10884.paper_summary: Automated generation of executable Business Process Model and Notation (BPMN) models from natural-language specifications is increasingly enabled by large language models. However, ambiguous or underspecified text can yield structurally valid models with different simulated behavior. Our goal is not to prove that one generated BPMN model is semantically correct, but to detect when a natural-language specification fails to support a stable executable interpretation under repeated generation and simulation. We present a diagnosis-driven framework that detects behavioral inconsistency from the empirical distribution of key performance indicators (KPIs), localizes divergence to gateway logic using model-based diagnosis, maps that logic back to verbatim narrative segments, and repairs the source text through evidence-based refinement. Experiments on diabetic nephropathy health-guidance policies show that the method reduces variability in regenerated model behavior. The result is a closed-loop approach for validating and repairing executable process specifications in the absence of ground-truth BPMN models.
2604.10884.paper_summary_zh: 自動生成可執行的業務流程模型與標記法（BPMN）模型，越來越依賴大型語言模型來處理自然語言規範。<lb/>然而，模糊或不明確的文本可能會產生結構上有效但模擬行為不同的模型。<lb/>我們的目標不是證明生成的BPMN模型在語義上是正確的，而是檢測當自然語言規範未能支持穩定的可執行解釋時的情況，這是在重複生成和模擬的過程中發生的。<lb/>我們提出了一個基於診斷的框架，該框架從關鍵績效指標（KPI）的實證分佈中檢測行為不一致，利用基於模型的診斷將偏差定位到網關邏輯，並將該邏輯映射回逐字的敘述片段，通過基於證據的精煉來修正源文本。<lb/>對糖尿病腎病健康指導政策的實驗顯示，該方法減少了再生模型行為的變異性。<lb/>其結果是一種在缺乏真實BPMN模型的情況下，用於驗證和修復可執行過程規範的閉環方法。
2604.10884.author: Ion Matei et.al.
2604.10884.authors: Ion Matei, Praveen Kumar Menaka Sekar, Maksym Zhenirovskyy, Hon Yung Wong, Sayuri Kohmura, Shinji Hotta, Akihiro Inomata
